curriculum.subject: Physics
curriculum.gradeLevel: Class XI / Class XII / 12th Grade / Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 19 units, 28 chapters, and 5 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand the need for standardization in measurements.
Differentiate between fundamental and derived units within the SI system.
Apply rules for significant figures to determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis to verify equations and derive relationships.
Define frame of reference and distinguish between scalar and vector quantities.
Apply calculus concepts (differentiation/integration) to describe motion.
Interpret velocity-time and position-time graphs for uniform and non-uniform motion.
Derive and apply equations of uniformly accelerated motion using graphical methods.
Perform vector addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components to solve 2D problems.
Analyze projectile motion by resolving initial velocity into horizontal and vertical components.
Describe uniform circular motion in terms of centripetal acceleration.

practicals.title: To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body using vernier calipers.
To measure the internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using vernier calipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of given wire using screw gauge.
To measure thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine the mass of a given object using a beam balance.
To find the weight of given body using the parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum plot L-T and graphs. Hence find the effective length of second's pendulum using appropriate length values.
To find the force constant of given helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using a sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension, for constant frequency, using a sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube by observing two resonance positions.

activities.title: To make a paper scale of given least count, e.g., 0.2cm, 0.5 cm.
To determine mass of a given body using a metre scale by principle of moments.
To plot a graph for a given set of data, with proper choice of scales and error bars.
To measure the force of limiting friction for rolling of a roller on a horizontal plane.
To study the variation in range of a projectile with angle of projection.
To study the conservation of energy of a ball rolling down on an inclined plane (using a double inclined plane).
To study dissipation of energy of a simple pendulum by plotting a graph between square of amplitude and time.
To observe change of state and plot a cooling curve for molten wax.
To observe and explain the effect of heating on a bi-metallic strip.
To note the change in level of liquid in a container on heating and interpret the observations.
To study the effect of detergent on surface tension of water by observing capillary rise.
To study the factors affecting the rate of loss of heat of a liquid.
To study the effect of load on depression of a suitably clamped metre scale loaded at (i) its end (ii) in the middle.
To observe the decrease in pressure with increase in velocity of a fluid.

projects.title: Study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends
Study variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR due to variation in power or distance of incandescent lamp
Find refractive indices of water and oil using plane mirror, equiconvex lens and adjustable object needle
Investigate relation between ratio of output/input voltage and number of turns in secondary/primary coil of a self-designed transformer
Investigate dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using hollow prism filled with different transparent fluids
Estimate charge induced on two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane using Coulomb's law
Study factor on which self-inductance of a coil depends by observing effect when put in series with resistor/bulb fed by AC source
Study earth's magnetic field using compass needle-bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer

marks_distribution: Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 2-3 hrs (Class XI/XII)
competency_weightage: Promotion of process-skills and problem-solving abilities
Application of Physics concepts to industrial/technological contexts

bloom_levels.level_1: Remembering: Recall facts, terms, basic concepts.
bloom_levels.level_2: Understanding: Organize, compare, translate, interpret ideas.

bloom_levels.level_3: Applying: Solve problems in new situations using acquired knowledge.

bloom_levels.level_4: Analysing/Creating/Evaluating: Examine parts, make judgments, propose solutions.

internal_assessment: Content excluded from NCERT textbooks for 2026-27 is not to be tested.
Schools may introduce internal variations in question paper templates while maintaining overall weightage.

class_xi_xii_general: Record of at least 8 Experiments (4 from Section A, 4 from Section B)
Record of at least 6 Activities (3 from Section A, 3 from Section B)

marks_breakdown.component: Two experiments one from each section
Viva on experiments, activities and project

project_assessment: Report of the project carried out by students (Marks: 3)

Suggested Investigatory Projects include studying internal resistance/EMF variations, LDR behavior with light intensity/distance, refractive indices using plane mirrors/lenses, transformer design, angle of deviation in hollow prisms, charge induction on Styrofoam balls, self-inductance dependence, and earth's magnetic field.

practicals.title: To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body using vernier calipers.
To measure the internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using vernier calipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of given wire using screw gauge.
To measure thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine the mass of a given object using a beam balance.
To find the weight of given body using the parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum plot L-T and graphs. Hence find the effective length of second's pendulum using appropriate length values.
To find the force constant of given helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using a sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension, for constant frequency, using a sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube by observing two resonance positions.

activities.title: To make a paper scale of given least count, e.g., 0.2cm, 0.5 cm.
To determine mass of a given body using a metre scale by principle of moments.
To plot a graph for a given set of data, with proper choice of scales and error bars.
To measure the force of limiting friction for rolling of a roller on a horizontal plane.
To study the variation in range of a projectile with angle of projection.
To study the conservation of energy of a ball rolling down on an inclined plane (using a double inclined plane).
To study dissipation of energy of a simple pendulum by plotting a graph between square of amplitude and time.
To observe change of state and plot a cooling curve for molten wax.
To observe and explain the effect of heating on a bi-metallic strip.
To note the change in level of liquid in a container on heating and interpret the observations.
To study the effect of detergent on surface tension of water by observing capillary rise.
To study the factors affecting the rate of loss of heat of a liquid.
To study the effect of load on depression of a suitably clamped metre scale loaded at (i) its end (ii) in the middle.
To observe the decrease in pressure with increase in velocity of a fluid.

projects.title: Study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends
Study variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR due to variation in power or distance of incandescent lamp
Find refractive indices of water and oil using plane mirror, equiconvex lens and adjustable object needle
Investigate relation between ratio of output/input voltage and number of turns in secondary/primary coil of a self-designed transformer
Investigate dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using hollow prism filled with different transparent fluids
Estimate charge induced on two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane using Coulomb's law
Study factor on which self-inductance of a coil depends by observing effect when put in series with resistor/bulb fed by AC source
Study earth's magnetic field using compass needle-bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer

marks_distribution: Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 2-3 hrs (Class XI/XII)
competency_weightage: Promotion of process-skills and problem-solving abilities
Application of Physics concepts to industrial/technological contexts

bloom_levels.level_1: Remembering: Recall facts, terms, basic concepts.
bloom_levels.level_2: Understanding: Organize, compare, translate, interpret ideas.

bloom_levels.level_3: Applying: Solve problems in new situations using acquired knowledge.

bloom_levels.level_4: Analysing/Creating/Evaluating: Examine parts, make judgments, propose solutions.

internal_assessment: Content excluded from NCERT textbooks for 2026-27 is not to be tested.
Schools may introduce internal variations in question paper templates while maintaining overall weightage.

class_xi_xii_general: Record of at least 8 Experiments (4 from Section A, 4 from Section B)
Record of at least 6 Activities (3 from Section A, 3 from Section B)

marks_breakdown.component: Two experiments one from each section
Viva on experiments, activities and project

project_assessment: Report of the project carried out by students (Marks: 3)

Suggested Investigatory Projects include studying internal resistance/EMF variations, LDR behavior with light intensity/distance, refractive indices using plane mirrors/lenses, transformer design, angle of deviation in hollow prisms, charge induction on Styrofoam balls, self-inductance dependence, and earth's magnetic field.

competency_groups.title: Physical World and Measurement - Units and Measurements
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to measurement systems, SI units, significant figures, uncertainty analysis, dimensional analysis, and their applications.
competencies: Identify fundamental and derived SI units for physical quantities
Apply rules of significant figures in calculations involving measured values
Determine the uncertainty in a result based on measurement precision
Perform dimensional analysis to verify equations and derive relationships between physical quantities

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Straight Line
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to describing motion using calculus, interpreting graphs of motion, and understanding uniformly accelerated motion.
competencies: Differentiate between position-time and velocity-time relationships using elementary concepts of differentiation and integration
Interpret graphical representations (v-t and x-t) for uniform and non-uniform motion
Apply equations of motion derived graphically or via calculus for uniformly accelerated motion

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Plane
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to vector operations, resolution of vectors, and analysis of projectile and circular motion.
competencies: Perform addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication of vectors using rectangular components
Calculate dot and cross products of vectors and apply them to physical problems
Analyze projectile motion and uniform circular motion as cases of two-dimensional acceleration

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion - Newton's Laws and Friction
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to understanding forces, inertia, momentum conservation, friction laws, and dynamics of circular motion.
competencies: Apply Newton's three laws of motion to analyze the equilibrium and acceleration of bodies under concurrent forces
Utilize the law of conservation of linear momentum in collision problems involving one or two dimensions
Distinguish between static, kinetic, and rolling friction and apply their respective laws

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power - Work-Energy Theorem
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to work done by forces, energy conservation principles, potential energy concepts, and collision dynamics.
competencies: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces using integration or graphical methods
Apply the work-energy theorem to solve problems involving kinetic and potential energy changes
Analyze elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions based on conservation laws

competency_groups.title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion - Dynamics of Rotation
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to center of mass motion, torque, angular momentum conservation, and rotational dynamics equations.
competencies: Determine the center of mass for systems of particles and rigid bodies (e.g., uniform rod)
Apply the principle of conservation of linear momentum and angular momentum in isolated systems
Relate linear motion variables to their rotational counterparts using moment of inertia and radius of gyration
competency_groups.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation - Universal Gravitation and Orbital Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Kepler's laws, gravitational fields, potential energy variations, and satellite motion.
competencies: Apply Newton's law of universal gravitation and Kepler's laws to planetary motion problems
Analyze the variation of acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth from Earth's surface
Calculate escape speed, orbital velocity, and total energy for satellites in orbit

competency_groups.title: Properties of Bulk Matter - Elasticity and Fluids
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to stress-strain relationships, Hooke's law, viscosity, Bernoulli's theorem, and surface tension.
competencies: Apply Hooke's Law and definitions of Young’s modulus, bulk modulus, and shear modulus to elastic behavior problems
Use Pascal's law in hydraulic systems and apply Bernoulli's theorem to fluid flow scenarios (e.g., Torricelli's law)
Analyze surface tension phenomena including capillary rise, excess pressure, and wetting properties

competency_groups.title: Thermal Properties - Heat Transfer and Thermodynamics Basics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to specific heat, calorimetry, phase changes, modes of heat transfer, and blackbody radiation.
competencies: Calculate heat exchange using specific heat capacities (Cp/Cv) and latent heats during state changes
Distinguish between conduction, convection, and radiation as mechanisms of heat transfer
Apply Wien's displacement law and Stefan-Boltzmann law to blackbody radiation problems

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics - Laws and Processes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to thermal equilibrium, state variables, internal energy, and thermodynamic processes.
competencies: Define temperature using the Zeroth law of thermodynamics and distinguish between heat, work, and internal energy
Apply the First Law of Thermodynamics to analyze isothermal, adiabatic, reversible, irreversible, and cyclic gaseous processes

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory of Gases - Molecular Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to kinetic theory assumptions, molecular speed distributions, degrees of freedom, and specific heats.
competencies: Derive the expression for pressure in terms of molecular motion using kinetic theory assumptions
Relate temperature to the root-mean-square (rms) speed of gas molecules
competency_groups.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations - Simple Harmonic Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to periodic motion characteristics, SHM equations, energy in oscillation, and pendulum dynamics.
competencies: Identify conditions for simple harmonic motion (SHM) and derive its differential equation from force laws
Analyze the relationship between uniform circular motion and SHM projections

competency_groups.title: Waves - Wave Properties and Superposition
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to wave types, superposition principle, standing waves, beats, and interference.
competencies: Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves and describe their propagation characteristics

competency_groups.title: Electrostatics - Electric Fields and Gauss's Law
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Coulomb's law, electric fields due to various charge distributions, flux, and applications of Gauss's theorem.
competencies: Apply the superposition principle to calculate forces between multiple point charges

competency_groups.title: Electrostatic Potential - Capacitance
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to electric potential, equipotential surfaces, dielectrics, capacitor combinations, and energy storage.
competencies: Calculate electric potential due to point charges, dipoles, and systems of charges

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity - Ohm's Law and Circuits
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to drift velocity, resistivity, internal resistance of cells, Kirchhoff's rules, and Wheatstone bridge.
competencies: Relate electric current to microscopic parameters like drift velocity and mobility

competency_groups.title: Magnetism - Magnetic Fields and Forces
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, forces on moving charges/currents, magnetic dipoles, and galvanometers.
competencies: Apply the Biot-Savart law and Ampere's circuital law to calculate magnetic fields for symmetric current distributions
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction - Faraday's Laws
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to induced EMF, Lenz's law, self-inductance, mutual inductance, and AC circuits.
competencies: Apply Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction to calculate induced emf
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Alternating Current - AC Circuits
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to RMS values, reactance, impedance in LCR circuits, resonance, and power factor.
competencies: Analyze series LCR circuits using phasor diagrams to determine phase relationships between voltage and current
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves - Spectrum and Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to displacement current, transverse nature of EM waves, and the electromagnetic spectrum.
competencies: Describe the characteristics of electromagnetic waves including their transverse nature

competency_groups.title: Optics - Ray Optics and Instruments
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to reflection, refraction, total internal reflection, lens formulas, and optical instruments.
competencies: Apply the mirror formula and lens maker's formula to solve problems involving spherical mirrors and thin lenses
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments

competency_groups.title: Wave Optics - Interference and Diffraction
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Huygen's principle, interference patterns (Young's experiment), diffraction phenomena.
competencies: Explain the phenomenon of interference using coherent sources and derive expressions for fringe width

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature - Photoelectric Effect and Matter Waves
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to particle nature of light, photoelectric equation, de-Broglie wavelength.
competencies: Explain the experimental observations of the photoelectric effect using Einstein's photoelectric equation
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atoms - Atomic Models and Spectra
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Rutherford's model, Bohr's postulates, hydrogen spectrum.
competencies: Apply Bohr's quantization conditions to calculate radius, velocity, and energy of electron orbits in hydrogen

competency_groups.title: Nuclei - Nuclear Properties and Reactions
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to nuclear composition, binding energy curve, fission, and fusion.
competencies: Analyze the variation of binding energy per nucleon with mass number to explain stability and nuclear reactions

competency_groups.title: Electronic Devices - Semiconductors and Diodes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to energy bands, p-n junctions, rectification.
competencies: Distinguish between intrinsic and extrinsic semiconductors based on doping
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits

marks_distribution: Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 2-3 hrs (Class XI/XII)
competency_weightage: Promotion of process-skills and problem-solving abilities
Application of Physics concepts to industrial/technological contexts

bloom_levels.level_1: Remembering: Recall facts, terms, basic concepts.
bloom_levels.level_2: Understanding: Organize, compare, translate, interpret ideas.

bloom_levels.level_3: Applying: Solve problems in new situations using acquired knowledge.

bloom_levels.level_4: Analysing/Creating/Evaluating: Examine parts, make judgments, propose solutions.

internal_assessment: Content excluded from NCERT textbooks for 2026-27 is not to be tested.
Schools may introduce internal variations in question paper templates while maintaining overall weightage.

class_xi_xii_general: Record of at least 8 Experiments (4 from Section A, 4 from Section B)
Record of at least 6 Activities (3 from Section A, 3 from Section B)

marks_breakdown.component: Two experiments one from each section
Viva on experiments, activities and project

project_assessment: Report of the project carried out by students (Marks: 3)

Suggested Investigatory Projects include studying internal resistance/EMF variations, LDR behavior with light intensity/distance, refractive indices using plane mirrors/lenses, transformer design, angle of deviation in hollow prisms, charge induction on Styrofoam balls, self-inductance dependence, and earth's magnetic field.

learning_outcomes.topic: Need for measurement, Units of measurement, Systems of units, SI units, Significant figures, Uncertainty in result, Dimensions of physical quantities, Dimensional analysis
outcomes: Understand the need for standardization in measurements.
Differentiate between fundamental and derived units within the SI system.
Apply rules for significant figures to determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis to verify equations and derive relationships.

learning_outcomes.topic: Frame of reference, Differentiation/Integration for motion, Uniform/non-uniform motion, Velocity-time graphs, Position-time graphs, Accelerated motion equations
outcomes: Define frame of reference and distinguish between scalar and vector quantities.
Apply calculus concepts (differentiation/integration) to describe motion.
Interpret velocity-time and position-time graphs for uniform and non-uniform motion.
Derive and apply equations of uniformly accelerated motion using graphical methods.

learning_outcomes.topic: Vector operations, Resolution of vectors, Projectile motion, Uniform circular motion
outcomes: Perform vector addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components to solve 2D problems.
Analyze projectile motion by resolving initial velocity into horizontal and vertical components.
Describe uniform circular motion in terms of centripetal acceleration.

learning_outcomes.topic: Force, Inertia, Newton's Laws, Momentum, Impulse, Friction (static/kinetic/rolling), Circular motion dynamics
outcomes: Explain the concepts of force and inertia using Newton's First Law.
Relate momentum change to impulse and apply conservation of linear momentum.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces.
Distinguish between static, kinetic, and rolling friction and their applications.

learning_outcomes.topic: Work done (constant/variable force), Kinetic energy, Work-energy theorem, Potential energy, Conservative forces, Collisions
outcomes: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces.
Apply the work-energy theorem to solve motion problems.
Distinguish between conservative and non-conservative forces.
Analyze elastic and inelastic collisions using conservation laws.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
learning_outcomes.topic: Centre of mass, Torque, Angular momentum, Equilibrium, Moment of inertia, Radius of gyration
outcomes: Determine the centre of mass for systems of particles and rigid bodies.
Apply conservation of angular momentum to rotational motion problems.
Calculate torque and moment of inertia for simple geometrical objects.
Compare linear and rotational motions using analogous equations.

learning_outcomes.topic: Kepler's laws, Universal law of gravitation, Acceleration due to gravity variation, Gravitational potential/energy, Orbital motion
outcomes: State and apply Kepler's laws of planetary motion.
Analyze the variation of acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth.
Calculate gravitational potential energy and escape speed for celestial bodies.
Derive expressions for orbital velocity and energy of satellites.

learning_outcomes.topic: Elasticity, Stress-strain relationship, Hooke's law, Moduli (Young's, Bulk, Shear), Poisson's ratio
outcomes: Define stress and strain and interpret the stress-strain curve.
Apply Hooke's Law to calculate deformation in elastic materials.
Differentiate between Young’s modulus, bulk modulus, and shear modulus.

learning_outcomes.topic: Fluid pressure, Pascal's law, Viscosity (Stokes' law), Bernoulli's theorem, Surface tension
outcomes: Apply Pascal's Law to hydraulic systems.
Analyze fluid flow types and apply Stokes' Law for terminal velocity.
Use Bernoulli's principle to explain dynamic lift and Torricelli's law.

learning_outcomes.topic: Heat, Temperature, Thermal expansion, Specific heat, Calorimetry, Latent heat, Heat transfer modes, Blackbody radiation
outcomes: Distinguish between heat and temperature.
Calculate specific heat capacities and latent heats for phase changes.
Compare conduction, convection, and radiation as heat transfer mechanisms.

learning_outcomes.topic: Zeroth/First/Second laws, State variables, Gaseous processes (isothermal, adiabatic)
outcomes: Define thermal equilibrium and temperature using the Zeroth Law.
Apply the First Law to analyze heat, work, and internal energy changes.
Distinguish between reversible/irreversible and isothermal/adiabatic processes.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases
learning_outcomes.topic: Equation of state, Pressure concept, RMS speed, Degrees of freedom, Equipartition theorem, Mean free path
outcomes: Derive the kinetic theory expression for gas pressure.
Relate temperature to the root-mean-square speed of molecules.
Apply the law of equipartition of energy to specific heat capacities.

learning_outcomes.topic: Periodic motion, Simple Harmonic Motion (S.H.M), Energy in S.H.M., Simple pendulum
outcomes: Identify characteristics of periodic motion.
Derive the equation of motion for a simple harmonic oscillator.
Analyze energy conservation in SHM and derive the time period of a simple pendulum.

learning_outcomes.topic: Transverse/Longitudinal waves, Superposition principle, Standing waves, Beats
outcomes: Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal wave motion.
Apply the principle of superposition to explain interference patterns.

learning_outcomes.topic: Conservation of charge, Coulomb's law, Superposition principle, Electric field lines, Dipole torque, Gauss's theorem applications
outcomes: Apply Coulomb's Law to calculate forces between point charges.
Use the superposition principle for systems with multiple charges.
Calculate electric fields due to continuous charge distributions using symmetry.

learning_outcomes.topic: Electric potential, Equipotential surfaces, Dielectrics, Capacitors (series/parallel), Energy stored in capacitors
outcomes: Calculate electric potential due to point charges and dipoles.
Analyze the effect of dielectric materials on capacitance.

learning_outcomes.topic: Drift velocity, Ohm's law, Resistivity/Conductivity, Internal resistance, Kirchhoff's rules, Wheatstone bridge
outcomes: Relate drift velocity to electric current and mobility.
Apply Kirchhoff's laws to solve complex circuit problems.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
learning_outcomes.topic: Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, Force on moving charges/conductors, Torque on current loop, Galvanometer conversion
outcomes: Calculate magnetic fields using Biot-Savart and Ampere's laws.
Analyze the force experienced by a charge or conductor in a magnetic field.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
learning_outcomes.topic: Bar magnet properties, Magnetic dipoles, Para/Dia/Ferro magnetic substances, Effect of temperature on magnetism
outcomes: Explain the behavior of materials in external magnetic fields.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
learning_outcomes.topic: Faraday's laws, Lenz's Law, Self/Mutual induction
outcomes: Apply Faraday's law to calculate induced EMF.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
learning_outcomes.topic: RMS/Peak values, Reactance/Impedance, LCR circuits, Resonance, Power factor, AC generator/Transformer
outcomes: Analyze series resonance in LCR circuits.

learning_outcomes.topic: Displacement current, EM wave characteristics, Transverse nature, EM spectrum and uses
outcomes: Describe the properties of electromagnetic waves.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
learning_outcomes.topic: Reflection, Refraction, Total internal reflection, Lenses (thin lens formula), Prisms, Microscopes/Telescopes
outcomes: Apply mirror and lens formulas to image formation problems.

learning_outcomes.topic: Huygen's principle, Interference (Young's experiment), Diffraction
outcomes: Explain interference and diffraction phenomena using wave theory.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
learning_outcomes.topic: Photoelectric effect, Einstein's equation, de-Broglie relation
outcomes: Explain the particle nature of light using photoelectric experiments.

learning_outcomes.topic: Rutherford's model, Bohr model (hydrogen), Hydrogen line spectra
outcomes: Derive expressions for radius, velocity, and energy of electron orbits.

learning_outcomes.topic: Nuclear composition, Mass defect, Binding energy, Fission/Fusion
outcomes: Analyze the variation of binding energy per nucleon with mass number.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
learning_outcomes.topic: Energy bands (conductors/semiconductors), p-n junction diode characteristics, Rectification
outcomes: Explain the working principle of a semiconductor diode as a rectifier.

activities.title: To make a paper scale of given least count, e.g., 0.2cm, 0.5 cm.
To determine mass of a given body using a metre scale by principle of moments.
To plot a graph for a given set of data, with proper choice of scales and error bars.
To measure the force of limiting friction for rolling of a roller on a horizontal plane.
To study the variation in range of a projectile with angle of projection.
To study the conservation of energy of a ball rolling down on an inclined plane (using a double inclined plane).
To study dissipation of energy of a simple pendulum by plotting a graph between square of amplitude and time.
To observe change of state and plot a cooling curve for molten wax.
To observe and explain the effect of heating on a bi-metallic strip.
To note the change in level of liquid in a container on heating and interpret the observations.
To study the effect of detergent on surface tension of water by observing capillary rise.
To study the factors affecting the rate of loss of heat of a liquid.
To study the effect of load on depression of a suitably clamped metre scale loaded at (i) its end (ii) in the middle.
To observe the decrease in pressure with increase in velocity of a fluid.

projects.title: Study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends
Study variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR due to variation in power or distance of incandescent lamp
Find refractive indices of water and oil using plane mirror, equiconvex lens and adjustable object needle
Investigate relation between ratio of output/input voltage and number of turns in secondary/primary coil of a self-designed transformer
Investigate dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using hollow prism filled with different transparent fluids
Estimate charge induced on two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane using Coulomb's law
Study factor on which self-inductance of a coil depends by observing effect when put in series with resistor/bulb fed by AC source
Study earth's magnetic field using compass needle-bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer

practicals.title: To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body using vernier calipers.
To measure the internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using vernier calipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of given wire using screw gauge.
To measure thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine the mass of a given object using a beam balance.
To find the weight of given body using the parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum plot L-T and graphs. Hence find the effective length of second's pendulum using appropriate length values.
To find the force constant of given helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using a sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension, for constant frequency, using a sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube by observing two resonance positions.
